claimText: Ordinary matter, also known as baryonic matter, interacts with electromagnetic radiation and is composed of baryons such as protons and neutrons.
evidenceExcerpt: Ordinary matter (baryonic matter) interacts with electromagnetic radiation and is composed of baryons like protons and neutrons.
examUse: Statement-based question distinguishing ordinary matter from dark matter based on electromagnetic interaction.
distractorHooks: Ordinary matter does not interact with electromagnetic radiation
Ordinary matter is primarily composed of dark energy

claimText: The majority of ordinary matter in the universe, approximately 76%, is distributed in the intergalactic medium (IGM) rather than within galaxies.
evidenceExcerpt: 76% of ordinary matter is distributed in the intergalactic medium (IGM), the thin gas between galaxies.
examUse: Factual statement in a multiple-choice question regarding the distribution of matter in the universe.
distractorHooks: The majority of ordinary matter is located within galaxies
Most ordinary matter is found in galaxy halos

claimText: Dark matter makes up about 27% of the cosmos and exerts gravitational force, but it remains invisible because it does not interact with light.
evidenceExcerpt: Dark matter makes up about 27% of the cosmos, does not interact with light, but exerts gravitational force and has mass.
examUse: Comparison question testing the properties of dark matter versus ordinary matter.
distractorHooks: Dark matter interacts with electromagnetic radiation
Dark matter does not exert gravitational force
Dark matter makes up the majority (over 70%) of the cosmos

claimText: Fast Radio Bursts (FRBs) are transient blasts of electromagnetic radiation in the form of radio waves that last only from milliseconds to seconds.
evidenceExcerpt: Fast Radio Bursts (FRBs) are blasts of electromagnetic radiation in the form of radio waves, lasting from milliseconds to seconds.
examUse: Statement-based question on the characteristics and duration of Fast Radio Bursts.
distractorHooks: FRBs are continuous radio signals lasting for years
FRBs are blasts of gamma-ray radiation

claimText: Fast Radio Bursts (FRBs) can be used to measure the distribution of matter across the cosmic web because they disperse into different wavelengths as they travel through space.
evidenceExcerpt: FRBs act as cosmic flashlights, dispersing into different wavelengths to measure matter distribution across the cosmic web.
examUse: Application-based question on how FRBs are utilized by astronomers to detect missing ordinary matter.
distractorHooks: FRBs maintain a single wavelength as they travel through the cosmic web
FRBs are used exclusively to map dark matter distribution
